summary: The reproduction bundle pins PF4J commit dfeabf010c87985e1ec7eaae2e2072c74dc52077 and uses a Java harness to verify that deletePlugin removes a plugin directory outside the configured plugin roots after loadPlugin(Path) stores an attacker-chosen path.
expected_security_impact: Running the bundle should show that loadPlugin(Path) accepts an out-of-root plugin directory and deletePlugin(String) then recursively removes that outside directory.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh cloned https://github.com/pf4j/pf4j.git, checked out dfeabf010c87985e1ec7eaae2e2072c74dc52077, and then failed at the docker build step because the host does not provide the docker binary. Separately, the direct Java harness succeeded against the same pinned commit during validation.
verification_method: Inside the container, verify.sh copies the checked-out repository to a writable workspace, compiles the pf4j module plus Pf4jDeleteOutsideRootsHarness.java, runs the harness, and requires the exact success string after confirming that the outside plugin directory no longer exists.
evidence_produced_by_run: artifacts/delete_runsh_attempt.log shows that run.sh cloned the target repository and checked out dfeabf010c87985e1ec7eaae2e2072c74dc52077 before failing at `docker build` with `docker: command not found`.
limitations_or_blockers: The session host lacks the `docker` executable required to build and run the reproduction container.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
